title: start script has no prestart guard or build dependency for dist/index.mjs
reasoning: The start script executes `bun dist/index.mjs` (line 26), which is the output of the build script (line 28, tsdown). There is no `prestart` script, no `prepare` hook, and no existence check to ensure the build artifact is present before execution. Running `bun start` on a fresh clone or after a clean will produce an opaque MODULE_NOT_FOUND error rather than a clear message directing the user to build first. For an autonomous agent handling real funds, a confusing cold-start failure could delay operators diagnosing deployment issues.
reproduction: git clone <repo> && cd prism-liquidity-agent && bun install && bun start  # fails: Cannot find module 'dist/index.mjs'
recommendation: Add a `prestart` script such as `"prestart": "test -f dist/index.mjs || (echo 'Run bun run build first' && exit 1)"` or simply `"prestart": "bun run build"` if a rebuild-on-start is acceptable.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for this feature, and unit/integration tests would not exercise the npm/bun script lifecycle or the presence of build artifacts.
suggestedRegressionTest: A CI smoke step that runs `bun run start --help` (or a no-op entrypoint check) on a fresh workspace before `bun run build` to assert a clear error, then after build to assert success.
minimumFixScope: Add a single `prestart` entry to the scripts object in package.json.
